entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A mathematical aiming system used in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: 3-cushion billiards
entities.description: A variant of billiards where the cue ball must contact three cushions before pocketing a ball.

entities.name: diamond markers
entities.description: Markers on the rails used in the diamond system for aiming calculations.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The ball struck by the player to hit other balls in billiards.

entities.name: object ball
entities.description: A ball that can be pocketed during a shot in billiards.

entities.name: rebound angle
entities.description: The angle at which a ball bounces off a cushion.

entities.name: spin
entities.description: A rotational motion imparted to a billiard ball.

entities.name: speed
entities.description: The velocity of a billiard ball.

entities.name: cloth conditions
entities.description: The state or quality of the table cloth in billiards.

entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A method for aiming in billiards.

entities.name: billiard table
entities.description: The playing surface of a billiard game.

entities.name: diamonds
entities.description: Reference points on the rail for aiming in billiards.

entities.name: plus 2 system
entities.description: A numbering system used in diamond system aiming.

entities.name: plus 3 system
entities.description: Another numbering system used in diamond system aiming.

entities.name: long rails
entities.description: The longer sides of a billiard table.

entities.name: short rails
entities.description: The shorter sides of a billiard table.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: Balls that are struck by the cue ball in billiards.

entities.name: plus 2 formula
entities.description: A diamond system pattern in 3-cushion billiards where the starting and ending diamond numbers sum to 2.

entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A method used in 3-cushion billiards for aiming shots based on diamond positions.

entities.name: long patterns
entities.description: Shots where the cue ball travels a significant distance across the table, typically involving the short rail and opposite long rail.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: short rail
entities.description: One of the rails on a billiard table, typically located near the corners.

entities.name: long rail
entities.description: The other rail on a billiard table, opposite the short rail.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: Balls used in billiards besides the cue ball.

entities.name: plus 3
entities.description: A formula used in billiards for shorter patterns where the cue ball travels less distance across the table.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The main striking object in billiards.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: Small balls that are hit by the cue ball during a shot in billiards.

entities.name: long rail
entities.description: One of the rails on a billiard table, typically longer than the short rail.

entities.name: short rail
entities.description: The shorter rail on a billiard table.

entities.name: corner
entities.description: One of the four corners of a billiard table.

entities.name: natural pattern
entities.description: A standard shot in billiards where the cue ball travels from one rail to another, typically hitting an object ball along the way.

entities.name: Cross-table shots
entities.description: A type of shot in 3-cushion billiards where the cue ball travels from one short rail to another, contacting one or more long rails.

entities.name: Diamond system
entities.description: A numbering system used for cross-table shots in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Short rail
entities.description: One of the rails on a billiard table, typically shorter than the long rails.

entities.name: Long rail
entities.description: One of the rails on a billiard table, typically longer than the short rails.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Object ball
entities.description: Any of the colored balls used in billiards, except the cue ball.

entities.name: Drill
entities.description: A practice session for developing billiards skills.

entities.name: Straight Plus-2 Patterns
entities.description: A drill focusing on shooting plus-2 patterns in a straight line.

entities.name: Angled Plus-2 Patterns
entities.description: A drill focusing on shooting plus-2 patterns at an angle.

entities.name: Plus-3 Patterns
entities.description: A drill focusing on shooting plus-3 patterns.

entities.name: Mixed Patterns
entities.description: A drill involving random positions and mixed systems of plus-2 or plus-3.

entities.name: Diamond System
entities.description: A system for calculating and executing shots in billiards.

entities.name: Cue Ball
entities.description: The ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Object Balls
entities.description: Balls that are struck by the cue ball in billiards.

entities.name: Diamonds
entities.description: Markers on the table used to set up shots in the diamond system.

entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A powerful tool for billiards that helps players make accurate shots.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: object ball
entities.description: A ball that is struck by the cue ball during a shot in billiards.

entities.name: cushion
entities.description: The boundary of the table where balls can hit and bounce back.

entities.name: spin
entities.description: A method of applying force to a ball in billiards, causing it to rotate.

entities.name: extreme angles
entities.description: Angles greater than 60 degrees when the cue ball needs to contact the cushion.

entities.name: very short shots
entities.description: Shots where the cue ball is very close to the first object ball.

entities.name: very long shots
entities.description: Shots where the cue ball needs to travel a long distance across the table.

entities.name: experience and 'feel'
entities.description: The ability of players to make adjustments based on their intuition and past experiences.

entities.name: table conditions
entities.description: Factors such as cloth speed, cushion hardness, and humidity that affect shot accuracy.

entities.name: intuition
entities.description: The ability to make quick decisions based on experience and understanding of the game.
